title: This is a Thing KYY X90G "Quad" Portable Monitor Review
gist: Review of the KYY X90G, a ~$479 folding accessory that surrounds a laptop with three 15.6-inch 1080p IPS panels to create a four-screen mobile setup, at the cost of significant weight, a separate power cable, and a non-plug-and-play driver install.

insights.claim: The KYY X90G is a triple-panel folding rig (three 15.6-inch 1920x1080 60Hz IPS displays) that clamps around a laptop to deliver four screens for mobile power users, normally priced at $479.
insights.speaker: Sam Sabinash
insights.so_what: Defines an emerging niche of multi-screen portable accessories above single-panel portable monitors; a small premium-priced segment.

insights.claim: The device weighs 1.6kg (about 3.5 lbs), roughly a 14-inch MacBook Pro, making it a carry-on rather than briefcase item and offsetting the portability premise of a travel monitor.
insights.speaker: Sam Sabinash
insights.so_what: Weight is the core adoption barrier for this category; competitors that cut mass could win the segment.

insights.claim: The X90G cannot run reliably off a single USB-C cable because it draws too much power and needs a separate dedicated power port and adapter, undercutting the one-cable convenience buyers expect from portable monitors.
insights.speaker: Sam Sabinash
insights.so_what: High power draw plus a required driver utility limits mainstream appeal to dedicated multi-screen users.
insights.quote: We were not having much luck getting this monitor to work with a single cable connection because it draws quite a bit of power. That was a bummer.
insights.locator: The other is a power port

insights.claim: Driving all three panels on a MacBook required installing a Silicon Motion utility rather than plug-and-play, and each panel is controlled independently rather than as one cohesive display.
insights.speaker: Sam Sabinash
insights.so_what: Reliance on DisplayLink-style software (Silicon Motion) signals the accessory depends on third-party display-driver chips, not native OS support.
insights.locator: The monitor also came with a USB drive
